skill.name: slide-making-skill
skill.description: Create and validate single-slide PowerPoint files with PptxGenJS using strict layout, theme, typography, and export rules.
skill.summary: Build polished native PowerPoint slides with PptxGenJS and focused QA guidance.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many apparent external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review shows they are Markdown code examples, API names, hex colors, or tutorial text rather than executable skill code. The real residual concerns are low-risk: the documentation recommends local npm and Python QA commands and shows example URL-based images and hyperlinks.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Command Usage
low_findings.description: The skill includes Markdown examples that tell users or agents to run local commands such as npm package installation and PowerPoint text extraction. These are legitimate slide-building and QA steps, but they still require normal command execution caution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly visible in Markdown documentation and are related to the skill purpose. They are not hidden, obfuscated, or connected to credential access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Example Network Resource References
low_findings.description: The PptxGenJS reference shows example.com URLs for slide images, hyperlinks, and backgrounds. These examples document API capabilities and do not target a suspicious endpoint, but generated slides could fetch remote media if users replace the URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are hardcoded URLs, but they use example.com and appear only in tutorial snippets. No evidence found of data transmission or unauthorized network behavior.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Labels Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance labels map to ordinary documentation text, Markdown headings, color strings, icon package names, and QA grep examples. No evidence found of cryptographic code, system inventory collection, or malicious reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no weak hashing, encryption, host discovery, or inventory logic at these cited locations. The matched terms are benign tutorial content.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern in Documentation Only
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis matched many JavaScript code fences and shell snippets as command execution. Review found documentation examples for generating slides and QA, not executable scripts bundled with the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious patterns are contained in Markdown examples and are not invoked automatically. The only shell commands support installation or validation workflows.

content.value_statement: Building native PowerPoint slides by hand can produce layout errors and corrupted files. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear PptxGenJS rules for slide structure, styling, export, and QA.
actual_capabilities: Defines a PptxGenJS single-slide file structure with createSlide and slideConfig exports.
Documents slide dimensions, positioning, text, shapes, images, tables, charts, and masters.
Provides strict theme, font, palette, page badge, and static-slide rules.
Explains common PptxGenJS corruption pitfalls, including hex color and shadow option issues.
Includes a QA workflow for extracting preview text and checking placeholder content.
limitations: It focuses on one slide file at a time, not full deck planning or narrative strategy.
It depends on PptxGenJS and related local tooling being available in the user environment.
It does not provide built-in image generation, asset licensing checks, or brand approval.
It gives implementation guidance, but visual quality still requires human review.

use_cases.title: Build a Branded Business Slide
use_cases.description: Create a native PowerPoint slide that follows a supplied color palette, font rules, and page badge requirements.

use_cases.title: Fix a Broken PptxGenJS Slide
use_cases.description: Review a slide script for layout mistakes, invalid colors, unsupported gradients, and export issues.

use_cases.title: Create Data Presentation Components
use_cases.description: Use documented chart, table, shape, and text APIs to build a clear presentation page with native PowerPoint objects.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one PptxGenJS slide for this topic: [topic]. Use a 16:9 layout, Arial text, and this palette: [palette].

prompt_templates.title: Match a Brand Palette
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a single slide using only these theme keys: primary, secondary, accent, light, bg. Include a page badge unless it is a cover slide.

prompt_templates.title: Repair Slide Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PptxGenJS slide file for corruption risks, unsupported styling, layout overflow, and missing exports. Then provide the corrected file.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Chart Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished chart slide from this dataset: [data]. Use native PptxGenJS chart objects, clean labels, and a QA checklist.

output_examples.input: A request for a cover slide about quarterly revenue performance using a five-color palette.
output_examples.output: A complete slide file with a centered title, approved fonts, theme-based colors, and a standalone preview export name.

output_examples.input: A slide script that uses eight-character shadow colors and a title that wraps badly.
output_examples.output: A corrected slide implementation that uses opacity properties, shrink-to-fit title handling, and a verification checklist.

output_examples.input: A dataset for quarterly sales with labels and values.
output_examples.output: A native PowerPoint chart slide with clean data labels, controlled chart colors, and readable axis styling.

best_practices: Provide the exact slide purpose, target audience, required palette, and slide index before generation.
Use only six-character hex colors without a leading hash symbol in PptxGenJS options.
Run a preview export and text extraction check before treating the slide as complete.
anti_patterns: Do not use gradients, animations, or transitions when the skill rules require static slides.
Do not reuse mutable option objects across multiple PptxGenJS calls.
Do not add remote images unless the source is trusted and allowed for the presentation.

faq.question: Does this skill create full presentations?
faq.answer: It is designed for single-slide PowerPoint page files. It can support deck workflows one slide at a time.

faq.question: Which library does it use?
faq.answer: It uses PptxGenJS, a JavaScript library for creating native PowerPoint files.

faq.question: Can it use custom fonts?
faq.answer: Yes, but the skill recommends system-safe fonts such as Arial and Microsoft YaHei for reliable rendering.

faq.question: Does it support charts and tables?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference includes PptxGenJS examples for tables, bar charts, line charts, and pie charts.

faq.question: Why are gradients discouraged?
faq.answer: The skill prioritizes reliable native PowerPoint output. It requires solid colors to reduce rendering and corruption issues.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low residual risk from documented command and URL examples, with no confirmed malicious behavior.
